patches.hypothesis: The bug is due to incorrect length checking in regionMatches causing out-of-bound exceptions. If the lengths are different, it should immediately return false.
patches.changes:
- return CharSequenceUtils.regionMatches(cs1, false, 0, cs2, 0, Math.max(cs1.length(), cs2.length()))
+ if(cs1.length() != cs2.length()) return false
+ iterate both sequences and compare characters
patches.diff:
- return CharSequenceUtils.regionMatches(cs1, false, 0, cs2, 0, Math.max(cs1.length(), cs2.length()))
+ if(cs1.length() != cs2.length()) return false
+ iterate both sequences and compare characters

patches.hypothesis: The buggy method fails because it uses the wrong length to compare sequences. When sequences have different lengths, it attempts to compare beyond the available index of the shorter one, causing an IndexOutOfBoundsException.
patches.changes: - Change Math.max to cs1.length() in the regionMatches method call
patches.diff: - Change Math.max to cs1.length() in the regionMatches method call

patches.hypothesis: The method incorrectly assumes identical length for two different CharSequences, leading to out-of-bounds errors during comparison. The fix needs to ensure comparison only within the length of the shorter CharSequence.
patches.changes: Replaced Math.max with Math.min to avoid out-of-bounds access.
patches.diff: Replaced Math.max with Math.min to avoid out-of-bounds access.

patches.hypothesis: The method is incorrectly comparing two CharSequences by always using the longer sequence length, leading to StringIndexOutOfBoundsException when the sequences have different lengths.
patches.changes:
- return CharSequenceUtils.regionMatches(cs1, false, 0, cs2, 0, Math.max(cs1.length(), cs2.length()));
+ return (cs1.length() == cs2.length()) && CharSequenceUtils.regionMatches(cs1, false, 0, cs2, 0, cs1.length());
patches.diff:
- return CharSequenceUtils.regionMatches(cs1, false, 0, cs2, 0, Math.max(cs1.length(), cs2.length()));
+ return (cs1.length() == cs2.length()) && CharSequenceUtils.regionMatches(cs1, false, 0, cs2, 0, cs1.length());
